intent: Double a number.
summary: Returns n + n.

intent: Triple a number.
summary: Returns 3 * n.

intent: Square a number.
summary: Returns n * n.

intent: Add one to a number.
summary: Returns n + 1.

intent: Negate a number.
summary: Returns -n.

intent: Quadruple a number.
summary: Returns 4 * n.

intent: Subtract one from a number.
summary: Returns n - 1.

intent: Absolute value of a number.
summary: Returns |n|.

intent: Add two numbers.
summary: Returns a + b.

intent: Multiply two numbers.
summary: Returns a * b.

intent: Subtract one number from another.
summary: Returns a - b.

intent: The larger of two numbers.
summary: Returns max(a, b).

intent: The smaller of two numbers.
summary: Returns min(a, b).

intent: The absolute difference of two numbers.
summary: Returns |a - b|.

intent: Test whether a number is positive.
summary: Returns true iff n > 0.

intent: Test whether a number is non-negative.
summary: Returns true iff n >= 0.

intent: Negate a boolean.
summary: Returns the logical negation of b.

intent: Logical AND of two booleans.
summary: Returns true iff both a and b are true.

intent: Logical OR of two booleans.
summary: Returns true iff a or b is true.

intent: Test whether a number is zero.
summary: Returns true iff n == 0.

intent: Exclusive OR of two booleans.
summary: Returns true iff exactly one of a, b is true.

intent: Test whether a number is even.
summary: Returns true iff n mod 2 == 0.

intent: Sum a list of numbers.
summary: Adds every element with foldl, 0 for the empty list.

views.surface_body: \xs -> foldl (\acc x -> acc + x) int(0) xs

intent: Reverse a list.
summary: Returns the elements in reverse order.

views.surface_type: forall a. List a -> List a

intent: Count the elements of a list.
summary: Returns how many elements the list has.

intent: Negate every number in a list.
summary: Maps neg over the list.

intent: Keep only the positive numbers in a list.
summary: Filters the list to its positive elements.

intent: Concatenate two lists.
summary: Appends the second list onto the first.

views.surface_type: forall a. List a -> List a -> List a

intent: Square every number in a list.
summary: Maps n*n over the list.

intent: Keep only the even numbers in a list.
summary: Filters the list to its even elements.

intent: Multiply all the numbers in a list.
summary: Folds mul over the list, 1 for the empty list.

views.surface_body: \xs -> foldl (\acc x -> acc * x) int(1) xs

intent: Count the positive numbers in a list.
summary: The length of the list's positive elements.

intent: Count the even numbers in a list.
summary: The length of the list's even elements.

intent: Sum the squares of a list of numbers.
summary: Folds add over each element squared, 0 for the empty list.

views.surface_body: \xs -> foldl (\acc x -> acc + x * x) int(0) xs

intent: Test whether every number in a list is positive.
summary: true for the empty list; otherwise (head > 0) and the rest are all positive.

views.surface_body: \xs -> foldr (\x acc -> x > int(0) && acc) true xs

intent: Test whether a list contains a negative number.
summary: false for the empty list; otherwise (head < 0) or the rest contains a negative.

views.surface_body: \xs -> foldr (\x acc -> x < int(0) || acc) false xs

intent: Test whether a list contains a zero.
summary: false for the empty list; otherwise (head == 0) or the rest contains a zero.

views.surface_body: \xs -> foldr (\x acc -> x == int(0) || acc) false xs

intent: Test whether every number in a list is even.
summary: true for the empty list; otherwise (head even) and the rest are all even.

views.surface_body: \xs -> foldr (\x acc -> x % int(2) == int(0) && acc) true xs

intent: Sum a list of numbers with a right fold.
summary: 0 for the empty list; otherwise head + the sum of the rest.

views.surface_body: \xs -> foldr (\x acc -> x + acc) int(0) xs

intent: Square a floating-point number.
summary: Returns x * x.

intent: Double a floating-point number.
summary: Returns x + x.

intent: Negate a floating-point number.
summary: Returns -x.

intent: Cube a floating-point number.
summary: Returns x * x * x.

intent: Divide two integers, returning nothing on division by zero.
summary: Just(a / b) when b is nonzero; None when b is zero.

views.surface_body: \a b -> case b == int(0) of { true => None; false => Just(a / b) }

intent: Modulo of two integers, returning nothing on a zero divisor.
summary: Just(a mod b) when b is nonzero; None when b is zero.

views.surface_body: \a b -> case b == int(0) of { true => None; false => Just(a % b) }

intent: The first element of a list, if it has one.
summary: Just(head xs) for a non-empty list; None for the empty list.

views.surface_body: \xs -> case null xs of { true => None; false => Just(head xs) }

intent: Divide two integers, reporting the divisor as an error when it is zero.
summary: Ok(a / b) when b is nonzero; Err(b) when b is zero.

views.surface_body: \a b -> case b == int(0) of { true => Err(b); false => Ok(a / b) }

intent: Subtract, erroring when the result would go negative.
summary: Ok(a - b) when a >= b; Err(b) otherwise.

views.surface_body: \a b -> case a >= b of { true => Ok(a - b); false => Err(b) }

intent: Count the elements of a list, by recursion.
summary: 0 for the empty list; otherwise 1 + the length of the tail.

views.surface_body: \xs -> case null xs of { true => int(0); false => int(1) + self (tail xs) }

intent: Sum a list of numbers, by recursion.
summary: 0 for the empty list; otherwise the head plus the sum of the tail.

views.surface_body: \xs -> case null xs of { true => int(0); false => head xs + self (tail xs) }

intent: Multiply a list of numbers, by recursion.
summary: 1 for the empty list; otherwise the head times the product of the tail.

views.surface_body: \xs -> case null xs of { true => int(1); false => head xs * self (tail xs) }

intent: The factorial of a non-negative integer.
summary: 1 when n is 0; otherwise n * factorial(n - 1).

views.surface_body: \n -> case n == int(0) of { true => int(1); false => n * self (n - int(1)) }

intent: The nth triangular number.
summary: 0 when n is 0; otherwise n + the (n-1)th triangular number.

views.surface_body: \n -> case n == int(0) of { true => int(0); false => n + self (n - int(1)) }

intent: Double every number in a list, by recursion.
summary: nil for the empty list; otherwise (2*head) consed onto doubling the tail.

views.surface_body: \xs -> case null xs of { true => nil; false => cons (int(2) * head xs) (self (tail xs)) }

intent: Add one to every number in a list, by recursion.
summary: nil for the empty list; otherwise (head+1) consed onto incrementing the tail.

views.surface_body: \xs -> case null xs of { true => nil; false => cons (head xs + int(1)) (self (tail xs)) }

intent: Negate every number in a list, by recursion.
summary: nil for the empty list; otherwise (-head) consed onto negating the tail.

views.surface_body: \xs -> case null xs of { true => nil; false => cons (-head xs) (self (tail xs)) }

intent: Square every number in a list, by recursion.
summary: nil for the empty list; otherwise (head*head) consed onto squaring the tail.

views.surface_body: \xs -> case null xs of { true => nil; false => cons (head xs * head xs) (self (tail xs)) }

intent: Concatenate two lists, by recursion on the first.
summary: the second list when the first is empty; otherwise head consed onto appending the tail.

views.surface_type: forall a. List a -> List a -> List a
views.surface_body: \xs ys -> case null xs of { true => ys; false => cons (head xs) (self (tail xs) ys) }

intent: Build the list n, n-1, …, 1 for a non-negative integer.
summary: nil when n is 0; otherwise n consed onto the countdown from n-1.

views.surface_body: \n -> case n == int(0) of { true => nil; false => cons n (self (n - int(1))) }

intent: Add three numbers.
summary: Returns (a + b) + c.

intent: Multiply a number by the sum of two others.
summary: Returns a * (b + c).

intent: Add zero to a number.
summary: Returns n + 0.

intent: Multiply a number by the difference of two others.
summary: Returns a * (b - c).

intent: Subtract a number from itself.
summary: Returns n - n.

intent: Logical AND of three booleans.
summary: Returns (a and b) and c.

intent: Logical OR of three booleans.
summary: Returns (a or b) or c.

intent: Logical NAND of two booleans.
summary: Returns not (a and b).

intent: Logical NOR of two booleans.
summary: Returns not (a or b).

intent: The maximum of a number with itself.
summary: Returns max(a, a).

intent: The maximum of three numbers.
summary: Returns max(max(a, b), c).

views.surface_body: \a b c -> max (max a b) c

intent: The minimum of three numbers.
summary: Returns min(min(a, b), c).

views.surface_body: \a b c -> min (min a b) c

intent: The maximum of two numbers, in either order.
summary: Returns max(a, b).

intent: The minimum of two numbers, in either order.
summary: Returns min(a, b).

intent: Ask an agent to compute double of 21.
summary: request/apply double to 21 → the responder runs it and asserts double(21) = 42, which re-runs true.

intent: Ask an agent to add 3 and 4.
summary: request/apply add to (3, 4) → the responder asserts add(3, 4) = 7, which re-runs true.

intent: Ask an agent to square 5.
summary: request/apply square to 5 → the responder asserts square(5) = 25, which re-runs true.

intent: Ask an agent to multiply 6 and 7.
summary: request/apply mul to (6, 7) → the responder asserts mul(6, 7) = 42, which re-runs true.

intent: Propose that an agent compute double of 21.
summary: propose/apply double to 21 → the responder test-runs it and commits.

intent: Ask an agent to validate the `double` function.
summary: request/validate double → the responder type-checks and runs it, then asserts it is verified.

intent: Delegate the capability to apply functions.
summary: delegate cap:apply → the responder acknowledges receipt.

intent: Offer to store the `double` function record.
summary: request/store the double record → the responder verifies its content-address and acks.

intent: Commit to computing double of 21.
summary: commit/apply double to 21 → the responder fulfils the commitment and asserts the result, which re-runs true.

intent: Withdraw an earlier request.
summary: retract a prior message → the responder acknowledges the retraction.

intent: Find functions that operate on lists.
summary: query for functions tagged `list` → the responder acks with the matching content-addresses.

intent: A function declared to return a bool, but whose body returns an int.
summary: Schema-valid and its example even runs, but the type checker rejects it: the body has type int, not bool.

verification.reason: ILL-TYPED: body has type (int) -> int, declared (int) -> bool — type mismatch: int vs bool

intent: A doubling function that wrongly claims double(n) = n + 1.
summary: The body and examples are correct, but the property is false; the prover refutes it with a counterexample.

intent: A doubling function whose worked example claims double(3) = 7.
summary: Well-typed, but executing the body against the example fails: double(3) is 6, not 7.

verification.reason:
example  0  FAIL  got {"kind":"int","value":6}  want {"kind":"int","value":7}
run: 1/1 examples failed

intent: A subtraction function that wrongly claims to be commutative.
summary: The body and example are correct, but a - b = b - a is false; the prover refutes it with a counterexample.

verification.reason: false_commutative: REFUTED      counterexample: ( (define-fun b () Int 1) (define-fun a () Int 0) )

intent: A reverse function whose worked example claims reverse([1,2,3]) = [1,2,3].
summary: Well-typed, but executing it fails: reverse([1,2,3]) is [3,2,1], not [1,2,3].

verification.reason:
example  0  FAIL  got {"elems":[{"kind":"int","value":3},{"kind":"int","value":2},{"kind":"int","value":1}],"kind":"list"}  want {"elems":[{"kind":"int","value":1},{"kind":"int","value":2},{"kind":"int","value":3}],"kind":"list"}
run: 1/1 examples failed

intent: A signed assertion claiming double(21) = 43.
summary: The signature is valid, but re-running the claim against the commons refutes it: double(21) is 42.

intent: A request to apply a capability-gated function without presenting the capability.
summary: The function requires cap:apply/guarded; the request lists none, so the responder rejects it as not_authorized.

body.reason: target requires capability [cap:apply/guarded] not presented in constraints.capabilities

verification.reason: {"code": "not_authorized", "reason": "target requires capability [cap:apply/guarded] not presented in constraints.capabilities", "rejects": "msg_ff6ab629480a9e9831b8df4a509471204239a8a40d247be26c69dc919bafa886"}

intent: Compose: reverse a list, then take its length.
summary: A two-stage pipeline reverse;length over List a, yielding nat.

intent: Compose: negate every element, then reverse the list.
summary: A two-stage pipeline negate_all;reverse over a list of ints.

intent: Compose: keep the positives, then count them.
summary: A two-stage pipeline keep_positives;length over a list of ints, yielding nat.

intent: Compose: square every element, then sum them.
summary: A two-stage pipeline square_all;sum over a list of ints, yielding int.

intent: Compose: keep the positives, square them, then sum.
summary: A three-stage pipeline keep_positives;square_all;sum — the sum of the squares of the positive elements.

intent: Compose: take a list's length, then reverse it.
summary: length yields a nat, which cannot feed reverse's List parameter — the pipeline does NOT compose.

composite.reason: stage 0's output type does not fit stage 1's input type

verification.reason: stage 0's output type does not fit stage 1's input type
